title: CommonJS utility files are incompatible with the package ESM mode
reasoning: With package.json declaring "type": "module", Node treats .js files as ES modules. These utility files use CommonJS globals require and module.exports, so importing or executing them as .js under this package will fail before the utilities can run.
reproduction: Run `node src/utils/logger.js` or import either utility from an ES module in this package; Node will throw because `require` is not defined in ES module scope.
recommendation: Convert these files to ESM syntax (`import` / `export`) or rename them to `.cjs` and ensure callers import them accordingly.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are linked for this feature, and package-level module loading is not exercised by the provided evidence.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a smoke test that imports `src/utils/logger.js` and `src/utils/notify.js` under the package's Node module settings.
minimumFixScope: Update both owned utility files or their extensions and corresponding import sites so the module format matches package.json.

history.reasoning: The original package ESM evidence still exists at package.json:5 with "type": "module". The CommonJS evidence also still exists in the same files, with only line drift: src/utils/logger.js still has require calls at lines 1-3 and module.exports at line 64; src/utils/notify.js still has require calls at lines 1-2 and module.exports at line 47. Current code also shows these utilities are still reached from other CommonJS-style files, e.g. src/index.js requires ./utils/logger, src/cron/reminders.js requires ../utils/notify, and multiple routes require logger/notify. Under the package-level ESM mode, these .js files remain incompatible with Node's ES module loader. Direct node/npm validation commands were blocked by the read-only execution policy, but the current source evidence is sufficient to revalidate the module-format mismatch as still open.
commands: node -e "import('./src/utils/logger.js').then(()=>console.log('logger import ok')).catch(e=>{console.error(e.name + ': ' + e.message); process.exit(1)})" (rejected by execution policy)
node -e "import('./src/utils/notify.js').then(()=>console.log('notify import ok')).catch(e=>{console.error(e.name + ': ' + e.message); process.exit(1)})" (rejected by execution policy)
npm run lint (rejected by execution policy)
npm run build (rejected by execution policy)
